notes: Centrino is an all-new integrated set of electronics: mobile Pentium 4 + chipset + 802.11 wireless adapter built from ground up for long battery life and performance
Intel launched $300M marketing campaign for Centrino including TV ads newspaper ads and Times Square billboards
Centrino is the most important introduction that Intel the industry's dominant microprocessor supplier will make in 2003
Intel spending tens of millions of dollars to accelerate a worldwide network of wireless access points (hotspots)
Centrino platform built from ground up for long battery life -- over five hours -- better than existing mobile Pentium 3 and Pentium 4 processors
All the major notebook computer companies are rolling out Centrino products at attractive mainstream prices
Notebooks represent a quarter or more of personal computer units sold to enterprises
Any employee can buy a $90 wireless access point at CompUSA and plug it into an RJ45 jack with a $3 CAT-5 cable creating 150ft unauthorized wireless coverage
One notebook supplier expects more than a quarter of business Centrino notebooks to ship with IT department instructions to remove the wireless radio mini-card at factory
Cisco making its LEAP protocol available to the industry; Centrino is LEAP-aware
Microsoft Windows Server 2003 can be hardened for wireless environments as Microsoft has proven on its own campus and elsewhere
PricewaterhouseCoopers says additional initial costs of wireless notebooks can achieve return on investment in six months or less
Aberdeen Group research: campus-bound meeting attendee can be productive an extra 30 minutes a day with wireless
Task workers such as physicians can see an hour a day in productivity improvements in conjunction with digital record-keeping
Kastner: Centrino notebooks and the wireless network they work best in should become one of the standard IT infrastructure building blocks
Banning wireless is about as effective as keeping hormone-charged teenagers apart; this artificial barricade will be impossible to police
The better solution is to face the inevitability and desirability of a wireless world and embrace it rather than ban it
Senior executives and sales people will quickly subscribe to public wireless services just like they were expensing cell phones before corporate policy
By 2005-2006 virtually all enterprise notebooks shipped with integrated Wi-Fi; Centrino became dominant notebook platform brand through 2008
Enterprise wireless bans were universally abandoned by 2005-2006; IT departments shifted to securing wireless rather than prohibiting it
Cisco LEAP found vulnerable to dictionary attacks by 2004; deprecated in favor of EAP-FAST and WPA2-Enterprise 802.1X; broader wireless security thesis proved correct via WPA/WPA2
Wi-Fi hotspots proliferated globally; by 2010 free Wi-Fi became standard in airports hotels and cafes; Intel's own wireless networking unit wound down but ecosystem thrived
Centrino integrated three components: mobile Pentium 4 processor + chipset + 802.11 wireless Ethernet adapter and radio into single platform
Core tension: 802.11 requires IT effort/money to secure but provides significant productivity gains; many IT shops opting out of wireless computing entirely
$90 consumer APs from retail stores create shadow IT risk: employees can create rogue access points exposing corporate network within 150ft radius
